testcases.input: nums = [1000,100,10,2] Output: "1000/(100/10/2)" Explanation: 1000/(100/10/2) = 1000/((100/10)/2) = 200 However, the bold parenthesis in "1000/((100/10)/2)" are redundant since they do not influence the operation priority. So you should return "1000/(100/10/2)". Other cases: 1000/(100/10)/2 = 50 1000/(100/(10/2)) = 50 1000/100/10/2 = 0.5 1000/100/(10/2) = 2
testcases.expectedOutput: "1000/(100/10/2)" Explanation: 1000/(100/10/2) = 1000/((100/10)/2) = 200 However, the bold parenthesis in "1000/((100/10)/2)" are redundant since they do not influence the operation priority. So you should return "1000/(100/10/2)". Other cases: 1000/(100/10)/2 = 50 1000/(100/(10/2)) = 50 1000/100/10/2 = 0.5 1000/100/(10/2) = 2

testcases.input: nums = [2,3,4] Output: "2/(3/4)" Explanation: (2/(3/4)) = 8/3 = 2.667 It can be shown that after trying all possibilities, we cannot get an expression with evaluation greater than 2.667
testcases.expectedOutput: "2/(3/4)" Explanation: (2/(3/4)) = 8/3 = 2.667 It can be shown that after trying all possibilities, we cannot get an expression with evaluation greater than 2.667
